anchor.snippet: a boundary-value problem is a differential equation subjected to constraints called boundary conditions

annotations.label: Solution to a boundary value problem

anchor.snippet: A solution to a boundary value problem is a solution to the differential equation which also satisfies the boundary conditions
a boundary value problem should be well posed

annotations.label: Boundary value vs initial value problem

anchor.snippet: A boundary value problem has conditions specified at the extremes
A boundary value is a data value that corresponds to a minimum or maximum input, internal, or output value specified for a system or component
Finding the temperature at all points of an iron bar with one end kept at absolute zero and the other end at the freezing point of water would be a boundary value problem
Concretely, an example of a boundary value problem (in one spatial dimension) is
A type 1 boundary condition, Dirichlet boundary condition
A type 2 boundary condition, Neumann boundary condition
A type 3 boundary condition is the Robin condition
then it is a Cauchy boundary condition
A type 0 boundary condition has no physical boundary
For an elliptic operator , one discusses elliptic boundary value problems
For a hyperbolic operator , one discusses hyperbolic boundary value problems
a common problem is to find a function which describes the electric potential of a given region
